briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet the bait. Once the fish is closer, speed up the cadence and they'll usually bite on the next pause.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A jerkbait is the best winter bait. Forward-facing sonar shows that long pauses are important not just because water is colder, but also to give bass time to come up from deeper water to meet
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on point, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Prefers the Shadow Rap Deep for its unique shimmy when paused - it slowly sinks with subtle shakes like a soft plastic stickbait, triggering bites on the pause. Less erratic than most jerkbai
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on point, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...
Johnston: For smallmouth bass in cold water, they seem to key in on tube jigs or a swimbait better than jerkbaits. For largemouth, suspending jerkbaits and deep-drop jigs are the go-to.

Palaniuk: 'For winter jerkbaits, use a smaller 70-size reel with 7.1:1 gear ratio in cold water, then bump up to 8.1:1 as it warms into spring. The smaller spool means even a faster ratio isn't too fast.'
Wheeler: For a spring pre-spawn event limited to three rods, picks a jerkbait/crankbait for deeper staging fish, a 1/2-oz jig for versatility, and a ChatterBait for stained shallow water.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
